description: Complete OpenTelemetry tracing coverage across all backends + workers. Closes ROADMAP P2 'TiKV meta backend emits no trace spans' + P3 'S3-over-S3 data backend emits no trace spans' AND fills the worker tracing gap noted in project CLAUDE.md (all 10 workers — gc / lifecycle / replicator / notify / access-log / inventory / audit-export / manifest-rewriter / quota-reconcile / usage-rollup — pass nil tracer today). Current coverage: HTTP middleware + Cassandra meta (`meta.cassandra.<table>.<op>` via gocql.QueryObserver) + RADOS data (`data.rados.<op>` via ObserveOp). Gaps: (1) TiKV meta — no observer; (2) S3-over-S3 data — no observer, AWS SDK calls untraced; (3) Workers — every worker emits nothing. Fix: stamp `strata.component="gateway"` on existing gateway-side observers first (clean baseline), then TiKV observer mirroring cassandra shape, S3 backend via otelaws AWS SDK middleware (auto-spans per SDK call with semconv rpc.* attrs), workers use existing `deps.Tracer.Tracer("strata.worker.<name>")` pattern + new `StartIteration / EndIteration` helper emitting `worker.<name>.tick` spans per iteration with attributes `strata.component="worker"` + `strata.worker=<name>` + atomic uint64 `strata.iteration_id`. Per-iteration only (no long-running parent). Worker sampling via same `STRATA_OTEL_SAMPLE_RATIO` tail-sampler; failing iterations always export (status=Error). NOTE: `workers.Dependencies.Tracer` is already `*strataotel.Provider` wired at `serverapp.go:254` — workers just need to USE it (no struct change). 6 stories. Closes ROADMAP P2 'TiKV meta backend emits no trace spans' + P3 'S3-over-S3 data backend emits no trace spans' on cycle close.

userStories.title: Gateway-side observers stamp strata.component=gateway
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every existing gateway-side span (HTTP middleware, Cassandra meta observer, RADOS data observer) tagged with `strata.component=gateway` so the Jaeger filter scheme rolled out in later stories works on day-one.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/otel/middleware.go` HTTP middleware: after `tracer.Start(ctx, ...)`, call `span.SetAttributes(attribute.String("strata.component", "gateway"))`
`internal/meta/cassandra/observer.go::SlowQueryObserver.ObserveQuery`: inside the span emission branch, stamp `strata.component=gateway`
`internal/data/rados/observer.go::ObserveOp`: inside the span emission branch, stamp `strata.component=gateway`

Add an exported constant `internal/otel.AttrComponentGateway = attribute.String("strata.component", "gateway")` AND `AttrComponentWorker = attribute.String("strata.component", "worker")` to avoid magic-string duplication across files
Unit tests in each of the three observers (`middleware_test.go`, `observer_test.go` for cassandra + rados) extended with one assertion: emitted span carries `strata.component=gateway`
Race detector clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: internal/otel/component.go exports AttrComponentKey + AttrComponentGateway + AttrComponentWorker. Middleware + cassandra emitSpan + rados ObserveOp all stamp strata.component=gateway. Tests extended: internal/otel/middleware_test.go (new), cassandra/observer_test.go (gateway assertion in wantAttrs), rados/observer_test.go (gateway pair in attr table).

userStories.title: TiKV meta backend observer + tracer wiring
userStories.description: As an operator inspecting a Jaeger waterfall on the lab-tikv stack, I want every TiKV transactional op to appear as a child span of its request so the meta-write step is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `internal/meta/tikv/observer.go` with `type Observer struct { logger *slog.Logger; tracer trace.Tracer }` and `WrapOp(ctx, op, table string, fn func(ctx) error) error` helper that starts a child span `meta.tikv.<table>.<op>`, runs `fn`, marks status from `err`, ends span. Attributes: `db.system="tikv"`, `db.operation=<op>`, `db.table=<table>`, `strata.component="gateway"` (via `internal/otel.AttrComponentGateway` from US-001)
Add `Tracer trace.Tracer` field to `internal/meta/tikv/config.go::Config` (matches `cassandra.SessionConfig.Tracer` shape)

`internal/meta/tikv/store.go` constructor stores tracer; every public Store method that hits TiKV (CreateBucket, GetBucket, PutObject, GetObject, DeleteObject, ListObjects, SavePart, CompleteMultipart, abort/complete/get*Multipart, SetBucketBlob/GetBucketBlob/DeleteBucketBlob, PutBucketStats/GetBucketStats — full enumeration via grep) wraps its inner txn body in `observer.WrapOp(ctx, "<op>", "<table>", func(ctx) error { ... })`
Wire `cfg.Tracer = tp.Tracer("strata.meta.tikv")` in `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildMetaStore` after `strataotel.Init` runs, mirror cassandra wiring at line ~545
Failing op (`err != nil`) sets span status to Error so tail-sampler exports
Race detector `go test -race ./internal/meta/tikv/...` clean
Unit test `internal/meta/tikv/observer_test.go`: fake tracer captures span name / attributes / status for synthetic op; success vs error paths verified
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: internal/meta/tikv/observer.go (already present) wires *Observer with NewObserver(tracer)/WrapOp/Start. internal/meta/tikv/config.go::Config.Tracer wired. Tracer wired in serverapp.buildMetaStore at the tikv branch via tp.Tracer("strata.meta.tikv"). Every public Store method that hits TiKV is now wrapped via s.observer.Start: buckets.go (Create/Get/Delete/List + updateBucket), objects.go (Put/Get/Delete/DeleteNullReplacement/SetObjectStorage), multipart.go (all 10 methods incl Complete/Abort/Record/Get/UpdateSSE), blobs.go (set/get/delete + Quota helpers + Inventory configs), bucket_stats.go (Get/Bump), iam.go (8 methods), managed_policies.go (8 methods), audit.go (8 methods), queues.go (GC/Notify/DLQ/Replication/AccessLog enq/list/ack), usage.go (Write/List/ListUser), reshard.go (Start/Get/Update/Complete/List), admin_jobs.go (Create/Get/Update), meta_health.go (MetaHealth). Tests: internal/meta/tikv/observer_test.go: WrapOp emits client-kind child span with db.system=tikv + db.tikv.table + db.operation + strata.component=gateway; error path flips status to Error; nil tracer is no-op; Store with backend-and-observer emits meta.tikv.buckets.CreateBucket span end-to-end.

userStories.title: S3 backend AWS SDK otelaws middleware
userStories.description: As an operator running an S3-over-S3 deployment, I want every AWS SDK call from `data.s3.Backend` to land as a span in the trace so PutChunk / GetChunk paths are visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/otelaws` as project dependency

`internal/data/s3/backend.go::connFor` installs `otelaws.AppendMiddlewares(&awsCfg.APIOptions)` BEFORE client construction. Spans emit per SDK call with auto-attributes `rpc.service=S3`, `rpc.method=<op>`, `aws.region=<region>`, `http.status_code=<code>`

Custom span-attribute hook stamps `strata.component="gateway"` (via `internal/otel.AttrComponentGateway` from US-001) + `strata.s3_cluster=<cluster-id>` on every span so traces are filterable per cluster

Add `Tracer trace.Tracer` field to `internal/data/s3/config.go::Config`; wire `cfg.Tracer = tp.Tracer("strata.data.s3")` in `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildDataBackend` for `case "s3":` (mirror rados wiring at line ~368)
If `cfg.Tracer == nil` (tests), skip middleware registration — preserves zero-config test fixture from `s3test.NewFixture`
Failing SDK call (`http.status_code >= 400`) marks span status Error so tail-sampler exports
Race detector `go test -race ./internal/data/s3/...` clean

Unit test `internal/data/s3/observer_test.go`: drive Backend.Put against httptest fake; assert otelaws-emitted span carries `rpc.method=PutObject`, `strata.component=gateway`, `strata.s3_cluster=<id>`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped: go.mod adds go.opentelemetry.io/contrib/instrumentation/github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/otelaws v0.68.0 — pulls otel sdk to v1.43.0 + smithy-go v1.24.3. internal/data/s3/observer.go exposes installOTelMiddleware + stampStrataAttrs(clusterID) which feeds an otelaws.AttributeBuilder stamping strata.component=gateway + strata.s3_cluster=<id>. internal/data/s3/backend.go Config grows Tracer trace.Tracer + TracerProvider trace.TracerProvider; resolveTracerProvider picks Config.TracerProvider > otel.GetTracerProvider() (when Tracer non-nil) > nil. Backend.tracerProvider plumbs through to connFor which calls installOTelMiddleware BEFORE the metrics instrumentStack so otelaws Initialize-after brackets every retry. legacy single-cluster Open() forwards cfg.Tracer + cfg.TracerProvider onto the synthesised newCfg so test fixtures using the legacy shape inherit tracing. internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildDataBackend case "s3" wires Tracer=tp.Tracer("strata.data.s3") + TracerProvider=tp.TracerProvider(). internal/otel/otel.go: bumped semconv v1.39.0 → v1.40.0 (matches sdk 1.43 resource.Default schema URL — gotcha pinned in CLAUDE.md + observability.md); new Provider.TracerProvider() accessor for instrumentation hooks that need the provider directly. internal/data/s3/observer_test.go: drives synthetic PutObject through the SDK + asserts the emitted client-kind span carries strata.component=gateway, strata.s3_cluster=default, rpc.method contains PutObject, rpc.system.name=aws-api; drives a NoSuchKey GetObject + asserts status=Error; zero-config (nil tracer + nil provider) asserts no spans emitted (existing fixtures stay hermetic). go vet + go build + targeted race tests across internal/data/s3, internal/data/s3/s3test, internal/serverapp, internal/otel, internal/meta/cassandra, internal/data/rados all PASS. Pre-existing flake in internal/lifecycle/TestThreeReplicaDistribution exists on baseline (verified via git stash -u) and is unrelated to this change.

userStories.title: Worker tracing — iteration helper + gc/lifecycle wiring
userStories.description: As an operator, I want each gc / lifecycle worker iteration to appear as a discrete trace so I can correlate scheduling decisions with their meta + data ops.
acceptanceCriteria: `workers.Dependencies.Tracer` is ALREADY `*strataotel.Provider` (wired at `serverapp.go:254`). No struct change needed — workers just need to USE it via `deps.Tracer.Tracer("strata.worker.<name>")`

Add `cmd/strata/workers/iteration_span.go` helper: `StartIteration(ctx context.Context, tracer trace.Tracer, workerName string) (context.Context, trace.Span)` returns child span named `worker.<name>.tick` with attributes `strata.component="worker"` (via `internal/otel.AttrComponentWorker`), `strata.worker=<name>`, `strata.iteration_id=<atomic-uint64-counter>` (per-worker, atomic.AddUint64 — safe for fan-out workers). `EndIteration(span trace.Span, err error)` marks Error on `err != nil` and ends span
If `tracer == nil` (unit tests without tracer wiring), iteration spans use `tracenoop.NewTracerProvider().Tracer(...)` — no-panic, no behavior change

`cmd/strata/workers/gc.go::Build` extracts `tracer := deps.Tracer.Tracer("strata.worker.gc")` and propagates into gc Runner; gc's main loop wraps each scan-batch tick in `StartIteration` / `EndIteration`. Child spans for `gc.scan_partition`, `gc.delete_chunk` emit under the parent iteration span via `tracer.Start`
`cmd/strata/workers/lifecycle.go::Build` same pattern: per-iteration parent span + child spans for `lifecycle.scan_bucket`, `lifecycle.expire_object`, `lifecycle.transition_object`
Sampling: worker spans flow through existing `internal/otel/sampler.go` tail-sampler; failing iterations always export (status=Error)
Unit test (gc + lifecycle): drive one iteration with fake meta backend that errors on third op; assert per-iteration span has Error status AND a `gc.delete_chunk` child span with Error status
Race detector clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Worker tracing — feature batch (replicator + 7 others)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every remaining worker wired to the same per-iteration span helper so trace coverage is uniform across all 10 workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 8 workers in `cmd/strata/workers/` (replicator.go, notify.go, access_log.go, inventory.go, audit_export.go, manifest_rewriter.go, quota_reconcile.go, usage_rollup.go) follows gc/lifecycle pattern from US-004: extract `tracer := deps.Tracer.Tracer("strata.worker.<name>")`, per-iteration parent span via `StartIteration` / `EndIteration`, child spans for per-tick sub-ops where meaningful (`notify.deliver_event`, `replicator.copy_object`, `access_log.flush_bucket`, `inventory.scan_bucket`, `audit_export.export_partition`, `manifest_rewriter.rewrite_bucket`, `quota_reconcile.scan_bucket`, `usage_rollup.sample_bucket`)
Each worker's test file (`*_test.go`) gains one trace assertion: drive single iteration, assert per-iteration span name + `strata.worker=<name>` + `strata.component=worker` attributes
No semantic behavior change — just span emission
Race detector `go test -race ./cmd/strata/workers/...` clean
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

acceptanceCriteria: Update `docs/site/content/best-practices/web-ui.md` (or add `docs/site/content/best-practices/tracing.md` if it does not yet exist as separate page — verify) with: full coverage matrix (HTTP middleware / TiKV meta / Cassandra meta / RADOS data / S3 data / Workers), span name conventions (`meta.<backend>.<op>`, `data.<backend>.<op>`, `worker.<name>.tick`), `strata.component=gateway|worker` filter examples for Jaeger, `strata.worker=<name>` per-worker filter examples, sampling behaviour (tail-sampler, failing spans always export)

ROADMAP.md close-flip in same commit per CLAUDE.md Roadmap maintenance rule: flip BOTH P2 entry 'TiKV meta backend emits no trace spans' AND P3 entry 'S3-over-S3 data backend emits no trace spans' to `~~**Px — ...**~~ — **Done.** ... (commit `<pending>`)`
